calendar.matters:
Petition For Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Feb-23-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Rueppel (Guardian Ad Litem for Ashley Neal) appeared in Propria Persona.
Constance Yu for Lawrence Cushman.
Scott McClain appeared by telephone for Diane Neal.

Ms. Yu stated that they reached a confidential settlement in August and that they preserved the issue of the petitioner having the right to petition for fees. Mr. Rueppel and Mr. McClain waive notice. The Court noted that the matter is unopposed and informed Counsel that is not approving the fees, but it is granting the petition pursuant to the settlement agreement. The Court granted the Petition for Attorney Fess and Costs (subject to receipt and review of the proposed order). Ms. Yu is to revise the order and circulate it for approval as to form. The Petition Compelling Trustee to Account, etc., Petition Denying Lawrence Cushman's Actions, etc. and Petition Confirming Actions of Lawrence Cushman, etc. are off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Denied

Petition For Order Denying Lawrence M Cushmans Actions For Order Removing Lawrence M Cushman As Trustee For Instructions To Distribute
Off Calendar Oct-24-2016 Continued To Dec-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Compelling Trustee To Account; To Instruct Trustee To Make Beneficiary Contact Information Available
Off Calendar Oct-24-2016 Continued To Dec-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Rueppel, Guardian ad Litem. Scott McClain (via court call) for Diane Cushman Neal.

The Court asked the parties where they stand. Mr. Rueppel stated that he was expecting Ms. Yu to be present, that Ms. Neal and Mr. Cushman have reached a settlement agreement, that there have been no petitions withdrawn as of yet, that Ms. Yu filed a fee petition which is set for November 3, 2016 but it is not showing on the Register of Actions. The Court recessed the case to give time for Ms. Yu to appear. The matter resumed, Mr. Rueppel stated that Ms. Yu misunderstood and thought the matter was under submission and that there has been a glitch with filing the fee petition. The court stated that it is concerned about setting another status hearing. Mr. McClain stated that they have reached settlement and signed the settlement agreement, that the fee petition is the only thing outstanding, that everything else has been resolved, that an accounting is not necessary and that they have provided information to the Guardian ad Litem. The Court stated that it will continue the Petition Compelling Trustee to Account, etc., The Petition for Order Denying Lawrence Cushman's Actions , etc., and the Petition for Order Confirming Actions of Lawrence Cushman, etc., were continued to December 5, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the understanding that the only remaining issue is the fee petition.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Confirming Actions Of Lawrence M Cushman And To Compel An Accounting Of The Former Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-24-2016 Continued To Dec-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Compelling Trustee To Account; To Instruct Trustee To Make Beneficiary Contact Information Available
Off Calendar Aug-22-2016 Continued To Oct-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Rueppel, Guardian ad Litem in Propria Persona. Constance Yu & Ellen Liu for Lawrence Cushman. Scott McClain & Keith Davidson for Diane Cushman Neal.

The Court stated that there were late filings, that it had no chance to review them, that the intent was to not have a hearing and asked if the parties wanted a new date or if the matter can be under submission. Ms. Yu reported that they have settled Lawrence & Diane's petitions, that the term sheets have been signed, that they are trying to resolve the Guardian ad Litem's petition, that the motion for attorney's fees will remain, that all of the terms will be executed in the next week or so, that they request a 30 day continuance for status and that if they get their motion filed the status can go off calendar. Mr. Rueppel stated that there may be an opportunity to view the accounting in the future and that he would like his petition continued for 60 days. The Court continued the Petition for order compelling trustee to account and to instruct trustee to make beneficiary contact information available, the Amended Petition for order confirming actions of Lawrence Cushman and to compel an accounting of the former trustee and the Petition for Order Denying Lawrence Cushman's actions, etc. to October 24, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Yu stated that the petition to resolve the matters will be separate.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Confirming Actions Of Lawrence M Cushman And To Compel An Accounting Of The Former Trustee
Off Calendar May-09-2016 Continued To Jun-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Constance Yu for (petitioner) Lawrence Cushman. Scott McClain (via court call) for (respondent) Diane Cushman Neal.

The Court asked if it could get an order after hearing. Ms. Yu stated that she met with Mr. McClain, that they want to resolve the issue with the guidance of the Probate Examiner's notes, that on 1/15/16 a Motion to Quash and a Motion to Stay was filed on behalf of Diane Cushman Neal, Zachary Neal and Ashley Neal, that most recently an objection was filed on behalf of Diane only and that they want to continue to meet and confer informally. Mr. McClain requested a continuance to late July, that it will give them time to continue discussions and that it will hopefully solve the Guardian ad Litem issues. Ms. Yu stated that if they are required to appoint a Guardian ad Litem they will be in a bind unless they can do it ex parte. Ms. Yu further reported that the Colorado action was dismissed on 4/19/16 and that they can file a copy of the order if necessary. The petition for order confirming actions of Lawrence M. Cushman and to compel an accounting of the former trustee was continued to June 27, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Confirming Actions Of Lawrence M Cushman And To Compel An Accounting Of The Former Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-06-2016 Continued To May-09-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Confirming Actions Of Lawrence M Cushman And To Compel An Accounting Of The Former Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-09-2016 Continued To Apr-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Diane Cushman Neal, Zachary Neal And Ashley Neal'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Scott McClain for Diane Cushman Neal, Zachary Neal & Ashley Neal. Constance Yu for (petitioner) Lawrence Cushman who is present in court.

Mr. McClain argued the motion to quash stating that P.C. 17000 & 17003 gives the court jurisdiction, that where 17003 & 17002 applies is the principle place of administration, that if that cannot be determined then it is where the trustee resides, that from 3/08 through 12/15 or the present the trust has been administered in Colorado, the trustee is in Colorado, the accounts and records are in Colorado, the trustee and the beneficiaries are all Colorado residents and that the only ties to California are the trust. Mr. McClain argued the motion to stay stating that time bar is not an issue, cited CRS 15-16-201 which gives Colorado jurisdiction over the trust as the trustee and beneficiaries are residents, that it is the principle place of business, that the trust is registered in Colorado, that there is a maximum fine of $1000.00 for not registering a trust within 30 days, that according to case law there is no time bar and that Colorado has jurisdiction over the trust. Mr. McClain reported that there is a parallel action in Colorado, that certain of the issues are broader, that the action was filed at the same time the action in California was filed, that there is currently a motion to dismiss with an opposition filed, that there will be no hearing date until a reply is filed, that the petition in Colorado deals with broader trust construction issues, that the court has leave to decide the matter and that no records or witnesses need to be called, that the other concern with continuing the California case is that there could be different determinations by the different courts, that the trust does not own California property but has an interest in a Delaware company that owns property in California and that in regards to the concentration of witnesses in California, if Colorado makes a determination as a matter of law no witnesses will be called. Ms. Yu stated that regarding the motion to quash the tentative is correct, that part of the analysis requires the court to assume that Diane is the trustee, that the trust is a grantor trust, that the settlor is the current trustee, that the administration is occurring in California, that there are California income producing properties and that the only thing that happens in Colorado is that the money gets spent. Ms. Yu also stated that the trust has always had administration in California, that Diane does reside in Colorado and that the trust was administered in Colorado until she was removed, that regardless of where the trustee resides the activity is in California, that all of the witnesses reside in California, that the cases Mr. McClain cites involve Swiss, Swedish & Norwegian nationals and that California law applies no matter where the trustee lives. Mr. McClain argued that Ms. Yu's statements do not encompass the entire picture and that everything points to the residence of the trustee which is Colorado. The Court requested the parties to submit their respective proposed orders to each other for review, to submit them to the court no later than March 2, 2016 and the motions will be under submission as of that date. The parties can file objections to the form of order if necessary.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Diane Cushman Neal Zachary Neal And Ashley Neal'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Action On Grounds Of Inconvenient Forum Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Denied

Petition For Order Confirming Actions Of Lawrence M Cushman And To Compel An Accounting Of The Former Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-20-2016 Continued To Mar-09-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
